rules.equipment: All equipment must be carried on foot, no motorised access
rules.specialRequirements: Activator must ascend the summit on foot. No vehicles allowed in the activation zone.

rules.equipment: Portable or mobile operation from within the park
rules.specialRequirements: Operator must be physically within park boundaries. Park-to-park (P2P) contacts count double.

rules.equipment: Portable operation from within the protected area
rules.specialRequirements: 44 QSOs required for a valid activation. Must be within official protected area boundaries.
3 QSOs on VHF or 13 on HF. No terrestrial repeaters. Tower must be in TOTA database.
No minimum QSO count for activation. Points depend on summit prominence and height.

awards.name: Mountain Explorer
awards.description: Multiple summits activated in one trip

rules.specialRequirements: Operator must be physically on a qualifying island. QSL card required for awards.

awards.name: Honour Roll
awards.description: Top tier of IOTA island chasers

rules.activationRadius: Within castle grounds or within sight
rules.specialRequirements: Operator must be at or near the castle/fortress. Photo documentation recommended.

rules.activationRadius: At or near the bunker structure
rules.specialRequirements: Operator must be at or in direct proximity of the bunker. Photo documentation encouraged.

rules.activationRadius: On or directly adjacent to the beach
rules.specialRequirements: Operator must be on or at the beach. Photo documentation encouraged.
Summit must have between 100m and 149m topographic prominence. Similar rules to SOTA.
Operator must be at or in direct proximity of the lighthouse. Based on the ARLHS World List of Lights.
Event takes place on the third full weekend of August each year. Registration required.

programs.name: Lagos y Lagunas On The Air
programs.nameDE: Seen und Lagunen On The Air

rules.specialRequirements: Operator must be within 200 metres of the water body shore.

awards.name: LLOTA Activator
awards.description: Based on number of lakes activated

awards.name: LLOTA Hunter
awards.description: Based on number of lakes worked

rules.specialRequirements: Castle must be in the AFCH/ÖVSV reference list. Photo documentation recommended.

awards.name: COTA-OE Diploma
awards.description: Various diploma levels for Austrian castles worked

programs.name: Wainwrights On The Air
programs.focus: Wainwright fells in the Lake District

rules.specialRequirements: Summits are the 214 Wainwrights plus additional Outlying Fells.

awards.name: WOTA Awards
awards.description: Awards based on Wainwrights activated and chased

awards.name: SiOTA Awards
awards.description: Awards for silos activated and worked

rules.specialRequirements: Often combined with annual Mühlentag (Mill Day) events.

awards.name: ECA Awards
awards.description: Awards via GMA system for English castles

awards.name: ELA Awards
awards.description: Awards via GMA system for English lighthouses

programs.name: ZL On The Air
programs.focus: Various reference types in New Zealand

awards.name: ZLOTA Awards
awards.description: Awards for New Zealand references activated and worked

rules.specialRequirements: Typically held as an annual event weekend.

programs.name: Keith Roget Memorial National Parks Award
programs.nameDE: Keith Roget Memorial National Parks Award

awards.name: KRMNPA Awards
awards.description: Awards for Australian national parks worked

rules.activationRadius: At or near the heritage site

awards.name: HOTA Awards
awards.description: Awards for heritage sites activated and worked

rules.equipment: Any setup from the house location

awards.name: HOTA-UK Awards
awards.description: Awards for houses activated and worked

rules.specialRequirements: Based on Ordnance Survey 10km grid squares.

awards.name: WAB Award
awards.description: Awards for collecting British grid squares

rules.specialRequirements: Based on Irish Grid reference squares.

awards.name: WAI Award
awards.description: Awards for collecting Irish grid squares

rules.equipment: Any setup at a scout group station
rules.specialRequirements: Annual event on the third full weekend of October. Organised by WOSM (World Organization of the Scout Movement).
Participants are children aged 10–15. Supervised by licensed operators.
Participants must be under 26 years old. Annual YOTA Month in December with special callsigns.

awards.name: YOTA Award
awards.description: Awards for working YOTA stations during December event

programs.name: Get On The Air

rules.equipment: Supervised station at Field Day events
rules.specialRequirements: GOTA station at ARRL Field Day for unlicensed or newly licensed operators. Supervised by a licensed control operator.

awards.name: GOTA Bonus Points
awards.description: Bonus points in Field Day scoring

programs.eventPeriod: Fourth full weekend of June (ARRL Field Day)

rules.specialRequirements: Annual event in September organised by WACRAL (World Association of Christian Radio Amateurs and Listeners).

awards.name: CHOTA Certificate
awards.description: Certificate for participation in the annual event

rules.specialRequirements: Operate from a lighthouse listed in the ARLHS World List of Lights
